title: Cirrus: Add e2e task w/ upstream netavark
body: Add CI task + setup for running (rootfull) podman e2e tests with both `netavark` and `aardvark-dns` installed.  The binaries are downloaded from the most recent successful branch-level CI runs on the respective repos.  This PR does *not* actually perform any actual testing with the binaries, this will be enabled in another future PR.

comments.body:
***Note***: The new task ends up being called `int podman fedora-35 root host-netavark` which github (on my screen) truncates to `int podman fedora-35 root host-...` - very much not ideal :disappointed: I'm open to suggestions on how to improve this.

edit: I'm just going to force a custom name, since it's only one task (no matrix).

Perhaps?  `int podman host-netavark fedora-35 root` to make it look better on the home page?

Tests aren't happy, but I don't think they're related to this change.  Otherwise LGTM.

> Tests aren't happy, but I don't think they're related to this change.

Yeah...too much wish-full `git push --force` on my part.  I'll make this into a draft/WIP while I work it out.
There is no reason to merge this until @baude implements this in his e2e testing right?

> There is no reason to merge this

Only reason I can think of to merge is to limit how much you (collectively) need to remember :grin:   The only down-side to merging early, is it will execute a duplicate (wasted) e2e task - so a few pennies of VM time.  Otherwise, however it makes sense to merge WRT brent's TBD PR is fine by me.
Force-pushed w/ added commit to also download/install aardvark-dns.
As discussed this is ready. @baude will merge this when he's ready WRT his PR which sensitizes the e2e tests to the new environment.
